items.title: Show HN: Run Claude Code Through Codex, Kimi, Grok, or Cursor
items.why_it_matters: This open-source proxy lets you route your Claude Code CLI commands through other LLM providers like Codex, Grok, or DeepSeek. Since you want to save on API costs while keeping the Claude Code terminal workflow, this tool gives you the actual plumbing to do it. Try installing this proxy today and routing a test session through Codex to see if you can cut your development costs.

items.title: Show HN: BDFL – Open-source supervisor for Codex and Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: BDFL is an open-source terminal supervisor designed specifically for Codex and Claude Code sessions. It lets you act as the supervisor by reviewing, versioning, and approving plans before isolated worker agents execute them, which fits perfectly with your goal of getting better at the direction part of vibe coding. Try running BDFL on your Next.js repo this week to plan and execute your next feature branch with tighter control over what the AI actually writes.

items.title: Show HN: Hibernate and restore Claude Code sessions across reboots
items.why_it_matters: This utility lets you hibernate and restore your active Claude Code sessions across system reboots or terminal closures. Instead of wasting tokens and time rebuilding context and explaining your Next.js project to Claude Code all over again after a break, you can spin it right back up where you left off. Try installing this tool and hibernating your session before you close your laptop tonight so you can jump straight back into shipping tomorrow morning.

items.title: Accidental data loss in Claude Code and OpenAI Codex: when AI deletes user files
items.why_it_matters: A new post highlights cases where agentic tools like Claude Code and Codex have accidentally deleted or overwritten local files during complex tasks. Since you are using these tools to build your Supabase and Next.js project, a rogue write command could wipe out unsaved progress. Make it a habit this week to run a quick `git commit` before launching any multi-file Claude Code sessions so you can instantly roll back if the AI makes a destructive mistake.
